Wikiwiki Wiki

A project wiki generated from durable repo knowledge.

Start here

Understand Wikiwiki without reading raw records.

A project wiki generated from durable repo knowledge. Start with curated guides, then browse source-backed user and developer knowledge when you need more detail.

Field guide

Start with the project story

5 records Articles

Public wiki pages curated from source records and project knowledge.

 Guide Guides

Curated paths through user orientation, project concepts, and maintainer context.

 8 records Concepts

Durable explanations, domain terms, product ideas, and system concepts.

 17 records Decisions

Product, architecture, workflow, and implementation decisions with rationale.

Maintainer context

Browse the source records

24 records Devlog

Meaningful changes and development milestones in chronological context.

 3 records Notes

Lightweight facts, reminders, and working context.

 7 records Symbols

Important code symbols and where maintainers should look first.

 2 records Links

Relationships between records, files, generated docs, and source concepts.

Featured

High-signal knowledge

ArticleFor users

Getting started

Install the npm package, set up a repo wiki, generate the site, and verify the first local output.

getting-startedinstructionshomepage
ArticleFor everyone

Project overview

Wikiwiki keeps local article-first project wikis beside the code, with structured records as the durable source of truth.

productoverviewhomepage
ArticleFor everyone

Agent setup

Install the Codex-compatible wk skill so agents can run the same local wiki protocol as the CLI.

agentcodexinstructions
ArticleFor users

FAQ and troubleshooting

Common fixes for npm installs, missing generated sites, Beads coordination, and npm Trusted Publishing releases.

faqtroubleshootingrelease
ArticleFor everyone

Workflows

Use status/spin for orientation, records for durable knowledge, render/site for output, and closeout for reviewable end-of-work packets.

workflowinstructionsmaintenance
Created with Wikiwiki.
